2605.13050.title: Context Training with Active Information Seeking
2605.13050.paper_summary: Most existing large language models (LLMs) are expensive to adapt after deployment, especially when a task requires newly produced information or niche domain knowledge. Recent work has shown that, by manipulating and optimizing their context, LLMs can be tailored to downstream tasks without updating their weights. However, most existing methods remain closed-loop, relying solely on the model's intrinsic knowledge. In this paper, we equip these context optimizers with Wikipedia search and browser tools for active information seeking. We show that naively adding these tools to a standard sequential context optimization pipeline can actually degrade performance compared to baselines. However, when paired with a search-based training procedure that maintains and prunes multiple candidate contexts, active information seeking delivers consistent and substantial gains. We demonstrate these improvements across diverse domains, including low-resource translation (Flores+), health scenarios (HealthBench), and reasoning-heavy tasks (LiveCodeBench and Humanity's Last Exam). Furthermore, our method proves to be data-efficient, robust across different hyperparameters, and capable of generating effective textual contexts that generalize well across different models.
2605.13050.paper_summary_zh: 大多數現有的大型語言模型（LLMs）在部署後的適應成本高昂，尤其當任務需要新產生的信息或特定領域知識時。最近的研究顯示，通過操控和優化其上下文，LLMs 可以在不更新其權重的情況下，針對下游任務進行調整。然而，大多數現有方法仍然是閉環的，僅依賴模型的內在知識。在本文中，我們為這些上下文優化器配備了維基百科搜索和瀏覽器工具，以進行主動信息尋求。我們顯示，天真地將這些工具添加到標準的順序上下文優化流程中，實際上會使性能相較於基準下降。然而，當與一種基於搜索的訓練程序配對時，該程序維持和修剪多個候選上下文，主動信息尋求能夠帶來一致且顯著的增益。我們在不同領域展示了這些改進，包括低資源翻譯（Flores+）、健康場景（HealthBench）和重推理任務（LiveCodeBench 和 Humanity's Last Exam）。此外，我們的方法證明具有數據效率，在不同超參數下穩健，並能生成有效的文本上下文，這些上下文在不同模型中具有良好的泛化能力。
2605.13050.author: Zeyu Huang et.al.
2605.13050.authors: Zeyu Huang, Adhiguna Kuncoro, Qixuan Feng, Jiajun Shen, Lucio Dery, Arthur Szlam, Marc'Aurelio Ranzato
